pr.title: Split data, rename Dolphin_SDK2 to Pad, split up CARD.s
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly organization PR that removed most contents from the monolithic asm/data.s and split those data blobs back into their owning assembly modules, renamed the Dolphin_SDK2 area to asm/dolphin/pad/Pad.s, and split the huge asm/dolphin/card/CARD.s into many CARD subsystem files. The change appears primarily structural rather than behavioral: it moved data tables, strings, jump tables, and CARD code into more granular files and updated obj_files.mk and function references accordingly.
what_changed: Deleted the old monolithic asm/dolphin/card/CARD.s file, which had 7465 deleted lines, and replaced it with per-component CARD assembly files such as CARDBios.s, CARDUnlock.s, CARDCheck.s, CARDMount.s, CARDFormat.s, CARDOpen.s, CARDStat.s, CARDRead.s, CARDBlock.s, CARDWrite.s, CARDCreate.s, CARDRdrw.s, CARDDir.s, CARDRename.s, and CARDDelete.s.
Removed almost all contents from asm/data.s, with 1491 deleted lines, indicating that previously centralized data was moved into module-local assembly files.

Added .section .data blocks to many owning assembly files. The diff excerpt shows MetroTRK tables such as lbl_80400788, jtbl_80400810, jtbl_8040082C, lbl_80400848, lbl_80400854, lbl_80400864, lbl_80400878, and lbl_804008B8 moved from asm/data.s into dispatch.s, msghndlr.s, targimpl.s, dolphin_trk.s, and dolphin_trk_glue.s.
Renamed or reorganized the Dolphin_SDK2 pad area into asm/dolphin/pad/Pad.s, with additional changes in Padclamp.s and downstream references in controller, baselib, and melee files.
Added module-local data sections to numerous Dolphin SDK assembly files, including OS, DVD, DSP, GX, VI, JPEG, MCC, and DB-related files.
Updated obj_files.mk with 16 additions and 6 deletions, consistent with adding the split CARD object files and renamed/reorganized Pad file.

Updated include/functions.h with 59 additions and 59 deletions, likely reflecting symbol ordering or renamed declarations after the split; the provided context does not include exact declaration names.
smart_moves: Moved data next to the code that references it instead of keeping all SDK data in asm/data.s. This makes future C decompilation easier because jump tables, string literals, static tables, and other data can be associated with their owning translation unit.

Split CARD.s along likely original SDK component boundaries, using names such as CARDBios, CARDUnlock, CARDCheck, CARDMount, CARDFormat, CARDOpen, CARDStat, CARDRead, CARDBlock, CARDWrite, CARDCreate, CARDRdrw, CARDDir, CARDRename, and CARDDelete. This creates smaller, targetable files for later matching work.

Preserved global labels and raw data contents while relocating them. The diff excerpt shows the same labels and table entries removed from asm/data.s and added under .section .data in specific modules, which is a low-risk organization strategy.
Used SDK-like subsystem paths under asm/dolphin/card and asm/dolphin/pad rather than anonymous or catch-all file names, improving searchability and future source reconstruction.
Kept jump tables named with the existing jtbl_ prefix where already known, such as jtbl_80400810 and jtbl_8040082C, instead of over-naming weakly identified data.
decomp_lessons: For large SDK areas, splitting monolithic assembly by library component before writing C can reduce cognitive load and make it clearer which data belongs to which translation unit.
When relocating data out of a global data file, keep labels, order, alignment, and literal values intact unless there is stronger evidence for renaming or typing them.
Place .section .data blocks at the end of the owning assembly file when moving static data from asm/data.s; the excerpt repeatedly shows code followed by a new .section .data block.
CARD is best treated as multiple SDK modules rather than one massive file. Future CARD decomp work should continue using fine-grained files corresponding to SDK responsibilities.

Renaming broad placeholder directories or files to known SDK module names, such as Dolphin_SDK2 to Pad, is valuable even without converting code to C because it aligns the project structure with the original library layout.
Build file updates must accompany assembly splitting so that object order and linkage remain correct; obj_files.mk was updated in the same PR.
naming_conventions: Use Dolphin SDK component-style filenames with canonical capitalization for CARD files: CARDBios.s, CARDBlock.s, CARDCheck.s, CARDCreate.s, CARDDelete.s, CARDDir.s, CARDFormat.s, CARDMount.s, CARDOpen.s, CARDRdrw.s, CARDRead.s, CARDRename.s, CARDStat.s, CARDUnlock.s, and CARDWrite.s.
Use Pad.s and Padclamp.s under asm/dolphin/pad rather than the vague Dolphin_SDK2 name.
Keep unresolved data labels as lbl_80xxxxxx when no stronger semantic name is proven.
Keep jump-table labels as jtbl_80xxxxxx when the data is clearly a jump table but no higher-level symbol name is known.
Use subsystem directories under asm/dolphin, such as card, pad, os, dvd, dsp, gx, vi, jpeg, and mcc, to mirror SDK ownership.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Relocate .data from asm/data.s into the assembly file that owns or references it, preserving labels and values exactly.
For switch dispatch tables, keep arrays of .4byte code labels in the owning function file and mark them with .global jtbl_* labels as shown for MetroTRK msghndlr.s.
For pointer tables, preserve NULL entries and exact ordering when moving from a monolithic data section into a module-local .data section.

For function dispatch tables, preserve references to both real functions and fallback labels, as shown by lbl_80400788 containing TRKDoReset, TRKDoVersions, TRKDoSupportMask, TRKDoCPUType, TRKDoReadMemory, TRKDoWriteMemory, TRKDoReadRegisters, TRKDoWriteRegisters, TRKDoFlushCache, TRKDoContinue, TRKDoStep, TRKDoStop, and fallback lbl_8032785C entries.
When splitting assembly, make the file structure match likely original object boundaries and then update obj_files.mk so the linker sees the new object list.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the supplied PR context.
The PR was merged quickly after creation, but the provided context contains no explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes.
follow_up_queries: Check obj_files.mk in PR 217 to confirm the exact object ordering used for the split CARD modules.
Inspect include/functions.h diff from PR 217 to identify whether declarations were renamed, reordered, or only path-adjusted.
Compare old asm/data.s labels against new module-local labels to build a map of data ownership by translation unit.
Review subsequent PRs touching asm/dolphin/card/*.s to see which split CARD files were later decompiled to C.
Search for later renames of lbl_80400788, jtbl_80400810, and other moved MetroTRK data labels to recover semantic names.
